methods.description: This method involves designing primers for PCR and sequencing based on reported editing genes in mitochondria and chloroplasts. The cDNAs from reverse-transcription of WT and transgenic lines are used as templates for PCR, and the products are sequenced to compare RNA editing between transgenic lines and WT.

steps.description: Design primers for PCR and sequencing according to the reported editing genes in mitochondria and chloroplasts.
steps.searchable_summary: design primers for PCR based on mitochondrial and chloroplast editing genes

steps.description: Use cDNAs obtained from reverse-transcription of the WT and transgenic lines as the template for PCR with different primers combinations.
steps.searchable_summary: use cDNAs from WT and transgenic lines as PCR templates
inputs: cDNAs from WT and transgenic lines

steps.description: Sequence the PCR products and compare the sequencing results of RNA editing of transgenic lines with those of the WT to detect changes in RNA editings.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence PCR products and compare RNA editing between transgenic lines and WT

steps.description: Perform three repeated experiments in different independent lines with biological duplications.
steps.searchable_summary: repeat experiments for validation with biological duplications

methods.description: This method involves preparing samples for SEM analysis by cutting seedlings, immersing them in glutaraldehyde buffer, dehydrating with ethyl alcohol, drying at the critical point, sputter coating with gold, and observing with a scanning electron microscope.

steps.description: Cut seedlings into 1 cm long sections and immerse mature spikelets in 2.5% glutaraldehyde buffer for 18 h at 4°C immediately after separation from the plants.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare plant samples by cutting and immersing in glutaraldehyde buffer

steps.description: Dehydrate samples using different concentrations of ethyl alcohol at room temperature every hour.
Replace the dehydrating agent to dry the samples at the critical point.
Sputter coat the samples with gold in an ion sputter.
Observe the samples with a scanning electron microscope.

methods.description: This method evaluates the PPR-RNA recognition model by analyzing the associations between PPR756 and its target genes, using match indices and considering the effects of RNA secondary structures.

steps.description: Analyze the associations between PPR756 and its three target genes based on the reported general recognition mechanism.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze PPR756 associations with target genes

steps.description: Calculate match indices between PPR756 and its targets atp6, ccmC, and nad7.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate match indices for PPR756 and target genes

steps.description: Evaluate the importance of the last 8 nucleotides in match alignments.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate importance of last 8 nucleotides in alignments

steps.description: Consider the effects of RNA secondary structures on the binding affinities of PPR proteins.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze effects of RNA secondary structures on PPR binding
